tasks.spec: You are a fix worker in a repair swarm for mcp-local-rag (a TypeScript MCP + CLI local RAG server over LanceDB). Your current working directory IS a dedicated git worktree of '/Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag' — edit repo files in place here. FIRST, run: ln -sfn /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/node_modules node_modules (the worktree has no node_modules of its own). Boundary: you own ONLY these paths: src/server/index.ts, src/server/tool-input.ts, src/server/__tests__/, src/__tests__/server/. Do not touch .git, do not run git commit/branch/push, do not reformat unrelated code. THREE CONFIRMED FIXES, verified against source by a prior review swarm: (1) In handleIngestData, the outer catch (src/server/index.ts:586-592) unconditionally wraps errors in `new Error(...)`, downgrading structured McpError (e.g. InvalidParams 'No chunks generated' from the internal handleIngestFile call) to InternalError at the MCP boundary. Add `if (error instanceof McpError) { console.error(...); throw error }` as the first statement of that catch, exactly mirroring the sibling pattern at src/server/index.ts:488-491. (2) The ingest_file dispatch (src/server/index.ts:214-216) casts arguments via `as unknown as IngestFileInput` with no boundary validation, and the handler never type-checks filePath before `args.filePath.toLowerCase()` (src/server/index.ts:344) — a missing/non-string filePath surfaces as a confusing TypeError-flavored InternalError. Add a parseIngestFileInput validator in src/server/tool-input.ts (filePath must be a non-empty string; throw McpError(InvalidParams, ...) like the existing parseQueryDocumentsInput/parseIngestDataInput), call it from the dispatcher, and keep the existing inline visual/visualQuality checks working (move them into the validator only if straightforward). (3) The unknown-tool default (src/server/index.ts:233-234) throws a plain Error; change it to `throw new McpError(ErrorCode.MethodNotFound, ...)`. For each fix, add or extend a test in the owned test directories that fails before your fix and passes after (follow the existing test patterns in src/server/__tests__/ and src/__tests__/server/, e.g. tool-input.test.ts). HOW TO RUN: ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit and ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/server/__tests__ src/__tests__/server — run both before finishing; they must pass. OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave all code changes uncommitted (the validator exports the patch). Write ./fix-summary.md with '# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', under 700 words. If you cannot complete a fix inside the owned files, do not make speculative edits — explain the blocker in fix-summary.md so the check fails with a useful reason. Hard rules: no invented behavior, mark assumptions, never edit outside the ownership list.

tasks.spec: You are a fix worker in a repair swarm for mcp-local-rag (a TypeScript MCP + CLI local RAG server over LanceDB). Your current working directory IS a dedicated git worktree of '/Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag' — edit repo files in place here. FIRST, run: ln -sfn /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/node_modules node_modules (the worktree has no node_modules of its own). Boundary: you own ONLY these paths: src/parser/pdf-extract.ts, src/parser/__tests__/. Do not touch .git, do not run git commit/branch/push, do not reformat unrelated code. TWO CONFIRMED FIXES (one code block), verified against source by a prior review swarm: in extractPdfPages, the per-page loop (src/parser/pdf-extract.ts:89-123) has (1) no per-page error tolerance — `doc.loadPage(i)`, `page.getBounds()`, `page.toStructuredText(...)`, and `JSON.parse(...)` are all bare, so ONE corrupt/truncated page throws out of the loop, the caller's catch (src/parser/index.ts:359) wraps it in FileOperationError, and the ENTIRE document's text is lost including already-parsed pages; and (2) no `page.destroy()` — every loaded page handle is a native mupdf/WASM allocation held until the whole Document is destroyed (contrast src/pdf-visual/detector.ts:306 and src/pdf-visual/renderer.ts:85, which both call `page.destroy?.()`). Fix: wrap the per-page body in try/catch/finally — on failure, console.warn with the 1-based page number and the error message, push an appropriate empty entry so downstream page indexing stays aligned (inspect how pageDataList and stextJsonList are consumed before choosing the empty shape), and continue with the next page; in finally, call `page.destroy?.()`. Preserve exact existing behavior for healthy pages. Add a test in src/parser/__tests__/ proving one failing page no longer destroys the whole extraction (mock or stub the mupdf document/page following the existing patterns in parsePdfPages.test.ts / parsePdf-destroy.test.ts) and that page handles are destroyed. HOW TO RUN: ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit and ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/parser/__tests__ — run both before finishing; they must pass. OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave all code changes uncommitted (the validator exports the patch). Write ./fix-summary.md with '# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', under 700 words. If you cannot complete the fix inside the owned files, do not make speculative edits — explain the blocker in fix-summary.md so the check fails with a useful reason. Hard rules: no invented behavior, mark assumptions, never edit outside the ownership list.
tasks.verified: typecheck plus the parser test suite pass inside the worktree, and the exported patch touches only pdf-extract.ts and its tests — proving per-page fault tolerance and page-handle cleanup without changing healthy-page extraction.

tasks.spec: You are a fix worker in a repair swarm for mcp-local-rag (a TypeScript MCP + CLI local RAG server over LanceDB; this store serves Meridian's knowledge corpora, so data integrity matters). Your current working directory IS a dedicated git worktree of '/Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag' — edit repo files in place here. FIRST, run: ln -sfn /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/node_modules node_modules (the worktree has no node_modules of its own). Boundary: you own ONLY these paths: src/vectordb/index.ts, src/vectordb/__tests__/. Do not touch .git, do not run git commit/branch/push, do not reformat unrelated code. TWO CONFIRMED FIXES, verified against source by a prior review swarm: (1) The FTS keyword-boost block (src/vectordb/index.ts:382-390) is guarded by ftsEnabled/queryText/hybridWeight but NOT by `results.length > 0` — when vector search returns zero results, `uniqueFilePaths` is empty and the code builds the invalid predicate `` `filePath` IN () ``, issuing a guaranteed-to-fail FTS query that logs a spurious error on every such search. Add `results.length > 0` to the guard condition. (2) First-insert TOCTOU: insertChunks (src/vectordb/index.ts:197-214) decides to create the table with only `if (!this.table)` and then calls `this.db.createTable(...)` with no existence re-check and no exist-ok mode — two processes initializing against a fresh DB both see no table, the createTable loser throws 'table already exists' (rethrown as DatabaseError at src/vectordb/index.ts:226) and its ENTIRE chunk batch is silently never inserted. Concurrent writers are a documented, supported scenario (see src/vectordb/optimize-lock.ts:4-10). Fix: in the !this.table branch, catch the already-exists failure from createTable (match LanceDB's actual error robustly — inspect node_modules/@lancedb/lancedb typings/behavior; prefer an official create-mode/exist_ok option if the installed version exposes one), then openTable and add() the records so the loser's batch is inserted rather than dropped. For each fix, add or extend a test in src/vectordb/__tests__/ that fails before and passes after (follow existing patterns in vectordb.test.ts / search-filters.test.ts; for the TOCTOU, simulating createTable throwing 'already exists' via a mock is acceptable). HOW TO RUN: ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit and ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/vectordb/__tests__ — run both before finishing; they must pass. OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave all code changes uncommitted (the validator exports the patch). Write ./fix-summary.md with '# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', under 700 words. If you cannot complete a fix inside the owned files, do not make speculative edits — explain the blocker in fix-summary.md so the check fails with a useful reason. Hard rules: no invented behavior, mark assumptions, never edit outside the ownership list.
tasks.verified: typecheck plus the vectordb test suite pass inside the worktree, and the exported patch touches only vectordb/index.ts and its tests — proving the empty-IN() guard and the create-table race no longer drop a concurrent first ingest.

tasks.spec: You are a fix worker in a repair swarm for mcp-local-rag (a TypeScript MCP + CLI local RAG server over LanceDB). Your current working directory IS a dedicated git worktree of '/Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag' — edit repo files in place here. FIRST, run: ln -sfn /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/node_modules node_modules (the worktree has no node_modules of its own). Boundary: you own ONLY these paths: src/embedder/index.ts, src/embedder/__tests__/. Do not touch .git, do not run git commit/branch/push, do not reformat unrelated code. ONE CONFIRMED FIX, verified against source by a prior review swarm: dispose() races an in-flight initialize(). dispose() nulls this.model and this.initPromise (src/embedder/index.ts:74-75) WITHOUT awaiting or fencing an in-flight initPromise, while initialize() assigns `this.model = await pipeline(...)` (src/embedder/index.ts:97) and ensureInitialized stores `this.initPromise = this.initialize()...` (src/embedder/index.ts:130). If dispose() is called while the first embed()/embedBatch() is still loading the model, the in-flight pipeline resolves AFTER dispose nulled the fields, so this.model becomes non-null again — a loaded model the caller believes freed (leak), and a subsequent embed() seeing model==null&&initPromise==null can start a SECOND initialize(), loading two pipelines with one orphaned. Fix with a disposal fence: e.g. an epoch/generation counter or disposed flag captured at initialize() start, so a resolution from a pre-dispose epoch never assigns this.model (dispose the late-arriving pipeline instead of installing it); have dispose() capture and await any in-flight initPromise (tolerating its failure) before releasing, then null the fields; ensure a post-dispose embed() cleanly re-initializes a fresh epoch (the class contract allows reuse after dispose — preserve that, and preserve the existing failure-clears-initPromise retry semantics at src/embedder/index.ts:130-134). Add a test in src/embedder/__tests__/ that fails before and passes after: follow the existing mock patterns in lazy-initialization.test.ts, use a deferred/controllable pipeline mock to interleave dispose() during init, and assert (a) no model remains installed after dispose settles, and (b) a subsequent embed() initializes exactly one fresh pipeline. HOW TO RUN: ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit and ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/embedder/__tests__ src/chunker/__tests__ — run both before finishing; they must pass (chunker consumes the embedder interface, so its suite guards against contract drift). OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave all code changes uncommitted (the validator exports the patch). Write ./fix-summary.md with '# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', under 700 words. If you cannot complete the fix inside the owned files, do not make speculative edits — explain the blocker in fix-summary.md so the check fails with a useful reason. Hard rules: no invented behavior, mark assumptions, never edit outside the ownership list.
